jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car in the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase, perfect character integration and economy, but routine at this point in the series

jokes.text: A man who holds the current record for least amount of faces, with none! Help me!
jokes.setup: Guinness Book of World Records introducing bizarre record
jokes.punchline: Man with no face desperately crying for help
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark absurdist humor - the casual presentation of horror is perfect Simpsons

jokes.text: You'll also see the world's smelliest tumor!
jokes.setup: Continuing the bizarre Guinness records theme
jokes.punchline: Another grotesque 'achievement'
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up to the faceless man joke but less shocking

jokes.text: Ew! These records used to be real accomplishments. Now they're just gross.
jokes.setup: Lisa reacting to the degraded state of record-keeping
jokes.punchline: Her typical intelligent social commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - the voice of reason amid absurdity

jokes.text: Plus, you'll meet a dog who can't predict anything.
jokes.setup: Expectation of a talented dog
jokes.punchline: A completely useless dog
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of expectations about 'talented' animals

jokes.text: Then three other things.
jokes.setup: Building up to more exciting content
jokes.punchline: Completely generic, lazy description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comedic anticlimax - the complete abandonment of effort is funny

jokes.text: What an age we live in. Oh, look at all that Sweet'N Low!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on supermodel's stomach contents
jokes.punchline: His fascination with artificial sweetener
jokes.explanation: Homer's odd priorities on display - focuses on sweetener not the voyeuristic nature

jokes.text: I remember television!
jokes.setup: Commercial mentions remembering the '50s
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's overly specific and obvious memory
jokes.explanation: Typical Grandpa non-sequitur - remembers the medium not the era

jokes.text: [Announcer] Actual year may vary, Consult calendar for current year,
jokes.setup: '50s-style restaurant advertising
jokes.punchline: Legal disclaimer undermining the premise
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of legal disclaimers that completely defeat advertising claims

jokes.text: Why don't we eat there tonight? - Nah. We'll go next month.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests visiting the new restaurant
jokes.punchline: Homer's typical procrastination
jokes.explanation: Relatable married couple dynamics but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Dennis the Menace? Yes. I was America's bad boy. I once hid my dad's hat!
jokes.setup: Dennis claims to be America's bad boy
jokes.punchline: His 'crime' is incredibly mild
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how tame 1950s 'rebellion' was compared to modern standards

jokes.text: And another time, I accidentally stepped in Mr. Wilson's flower bed. That was a two-part episode.
jokes.setup: Building on the mild misbehavior theme
jokes.punchline: Such minor incidents warranted two-part episodes
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - the mundane incident getting dramatic treatment

jokes.text: Allen Gaines Burgers 'Un-American Cheese Sandwich'? 'Polio Dogs'?
jokes.setup: Reading the '50s themed menu
jokes.punchline: Menu items referencing McCarthyism and disease
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining '50s nostalgia with dark historical reality

jokes.text: It's clever how the names remind you of the '50s and at the same time, tell you what there is to eat.
jokes.setup: Lisa analyzing the menu design
jokes.punchline: Her overly analytical approach to themed dining
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa over-intellectualizing simple things

jokes.text: I feel like I've gone back in a time machine. - Dad, they have those everywhere.
jokes.setup: Homer amazed by the jukebox
jokes.punchline: Lisa's deflating reality check
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's wonder and Lisa's practicality

jokes.text: What an age we live in. [Chuckling]
jokes.setup: Homer's second use of the phrase
jokes.punchline: Repetition of his earlier amazement
jokes.explanation: Callback establishes Homer's repetitive amazement as a running theme

jokes.text: Oh, my throat doctor says I'm not supposed to do that anymore.
jokes.setup: Host does a wolf howl then immediately regrets it
jokes.punchline: Medical restriction on his signature move
jokes.explanation: Good aging performer joke - body can't keep up with the act

jokes.text: Don't try and make me. If I have to get a divorce, I will!
jokes.setup: Dancing contest announced
jokes.punchline: Homer's extreme threat over dancing
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation showing Homer's irrational priorities

jokes.text: Our grand prize tonight is a vintage 1955 Harley-Davidson motorcycle, - I need a dance partner!
jokes.setup: Homer refusing to dance
jokes.punchline: Instantly changes his mind for the prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - principles abandoned for material gain

jokes.text: Well, well, well. I have never seen such reckless disregard for a wife's well-being in my life. You just won yourselves a motorcycle!
jokes.setup: Criticizing Homer's treatment of Marge
jokes.punchline: Rewarding the behavior he just condemned
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic twist - moral condemnation immediately followed by reward

jokes.text: Finally I've won the respect of my fellow m- Get away from it!
jokes.setup: Homer's moment of triumph
jokes.punchline: Immediately becomes possessive and paranoid
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's instant shift from gratitude to greed

jokes.text: Well, that dream is over. At least we still have each other. Right, honey? Honey? [Wind Blowing]
jokes.setup: Marge seeking comfort after the chaos
jokes.punchline: Homer has already left her for the motorcycle
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat - the wind emphasizes her abandonment

jokes.text: Man, you're bending the hell out of that kickstand, Dad. Why don't you just take it for a ride? - Promise you won't laugh? - Yeah, I promise. - I don't know how. - [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Bart asks why Homer doesn't ride the motorcycle
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals he can't ride, Bart immediately breaks his promise
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with the added comedy of Bart's immediate betrayal

jokes.text: You're kidding! You don't know how!
jokes.setup: Bart's continued laughter at Homer's admission
jokes.punchline: His incredulous repetition
jokes.explanation: Extension of the previous joke but adds little new comedic value

jokes.text: If you weren't a man And my father too I'd buy you a diamond ring And then I'd marry you
jokes.setup: Touching father-son bonding song
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately romantic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of sweet father-son moment into uncomfortable territory

jokes.text: Today we'll be sitting quietly in the dark because teacher has a hangover. It's like a chainsaw in my head!
jokes.setup: Teacher explaining the day's activities
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate admission of her drinking problem
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing unprofessional teacher behavior

jokes.text: Remember to rebel against authority, kids! - Don't listen to him, children. - We already did.
jokes.setup: Homer giving bad advice, Skinner trying to counter it
jokes.punchline: Kids have already chosen to follow Homer
jokes.explanation: Nice irony - authority figure defeated by anti-authority message

jokes.text: The sermon today is on John 4:13. I think it was Jesus who said, 'Blessed are those'- Oh, the heck with it. Church dismissed.
jokes.setup: Reverend trying to deliver sermon despite motorcycle noise
jokes.punchline: Gives up entirely and dismisses church
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from attempted professionalism to complete surrender

jokes.text: Quiet, Marge. The motorcycle and I are trying to sleep.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to turn off the engine
jokes.punchline: Homer treating the motorcycle like a person
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's unhealthy obsession with the motorcycle

jokes.text: He won't do his homework, he only salutes the flag with one finger and he comes home every night with other people's blood on his shirt.
jokes.setup: Mother listing her son's rebellious behavior
jokes.punchline: Escalates from mild to extremely violent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from typical teen rebellion to homicidal behavior

jokes.text: Look what you've done to beautiful display! - Yeah, that's the life for me, Marge- cruisin' and hasslin' shopkeepers.
jokes.setup: Homer has apparently caused property damage
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual attitude toward petty crime
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's slide into delinquent behavior

jokes.text: Nothing can defeat a motorcycle gang! A gang. That's the answer.
jokes.setup: TV show about motorcycle gangs
jokes.punchline: Homer gets inspired to form his own gang
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot through Homer's TV-inspired epiphany

jokes.text: This gang's for rebels, Flanders, not 'conformos.' - Yeah! - Buzz off!
jokes.setup: Ned asking to join the gang
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up word 'conformos'
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at tough guy slang creates a silly neologism

jokes.text: Well, if I were a member, we could use my rumpus room as our lair. - That guy just don't get it, do he? - My rumpus room with the new bumper pool table? - Bumper pool? - I'm there.
jokes.setup: Gang rejecting Ned's offer
jokes.punchline: Immediately accepting when they hear about bumper pool
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing the gang's suburban priorities

jokes.text: I move we reconsider our club name. Make it something a little less blasphemous. After all, we don't want to go to hell.
jokes.setup: Ned concerned about the gang name 'Hell's Satans'
jokes.punchline: His literal fear of damnation from a club name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - religious concerns about fictional gang identity

jokes.text: How about the Devil's Pals? - No. You see- - Or the Christ Punchers? - The Christ- I don't think you understand my objection.
jokes.setup: Ned objecting to blasphemous names
jokes.punchline: Suggestions getting progressively more offensive
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy - each suggestion worse than the last

jokes.text: Whoa! 'Machine wash warm.' 'Tumble dry.' Ooh, la, la.
jokes.setup: Gang member examining their leather jackets
jokes.punchline: Excited by the care instructions
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of tough biker image with domestic practicality

jokes.text: 'Hell's Satans,' huh? I'm watching you punks. - Can't hassle us, pig. We're goin' the speed limit. Oink, oink, oink.
jokes.setup: Police chief confronting the biker gang
jokes.punchline: Homer's rebellion is following traffic laws
jokes.explanation: Great irony - ultimate rebellion is law-abiding behavior

jokes.text: You can lock us away, but you'll never defeat the Cobras. - Cobras? I thought you were the Hell's Satans. - Uh- Oink, oink, oink!
jokes.setup: Homer quoting the TV show about motorcycle gangs
jokes.punchline: Uses wrong gang name, gets confused, reverts to pig noises
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's confusion and reliance on TV dialogue

jokes.text: He's got a broom! - Let's get outta here! - Forget the pennies! Go! Go!
jokes.setup: Tough biker gang confronting Apu
jokes.punchline: Fleeing in terror from a cleaning implement
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of tough guy image - afraid of household items

jokes.text: You promised me no more brooms. - I know this is not your way. But we're in America now.
jokes.setup: Apu's wife confronting him about using the broom
jokes.punchline: Cultural adaptation justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Apu's adaptation to American assertiveness

jokes.text: Her turn-ons include thievery and liquor. Her turnoffs include underpants, pedestrians and justice.
jokes.setup: Homer reading Marge's fake biker profile
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous criminal personality traits
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist list - 'pedestrians' as a turnoff is particularly funny

jokes.text: You stole our club name. According to our bylaws, we gotta stomp you.
jokes.setup: Real motorcycle gang confronting Homer's fake gang
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic approach to violent retribution
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of violence with formal procedures

jokes.text: Hey, hey! Chew with your mouth closed, please. - Sorry.
jokes.setup: Homer being forced to eat his jacket
jokes.punchline: Biker concerned with table manners during punishment
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between violence and politeness

jokes.text: Now you gotta eat that too! - You got anything else with our logo on it? - Caps and Frisbees, sir. - You gotta eat them too! - And some pogs.
jokes.setup: Homer being forced to eat gang merchandise
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly difficult items to consume, ending with dated pogs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the dated reference to pogs adding humor

jokes.text: Forget it, Simpson. Those pig noises you made really hurt my feelings. Lookin' like a pig as I do.
jokes.setup: Wiggum refusing to help Homer
jokes.punchline: Personal offense at pig noises due to his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment and callback showing Wiggum's self-awareness

jokes.text: But you have so much inner beauty. - Well, uh, be that as it may... uh, the gang is wanted in eight other states. And we have a little saying around here- 'Let Michigan handle it.'
jokes.setup: Homer trying to flatter Wiggum
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's policy of passing problems to other states
jokes.explanation: Great satire of jurisdictional buck-passing in law enforcement

jokes.text: I'd kill for some waffles. - He has. Remember the IHOP in Oakland?
jokes.setup: Casual expression about wanting waffles
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation revealing actual murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - perfect setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: Marge, how did you get my jacket so clean? I've tried everything to get those blood and puke stains out. I've tried hitting 'em. I've tried yelling at 'em.
jokes.setup: Biker asking about cleaning methods
jokes.punchline: Violence as attempted cleaning technique
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing biker's limited problem-solving skills

jokes.text: Hey! Hey! That's a leave-in conditioner! You're done. Next!
jokes.setup: Marge washing the bikers' hair
jokes.punchline: Professional salon-like efficiency
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's domestic efficiency

jokes.text: Dad, I'm tired of bathing in the yard. Plus, I think Rod and Todd are watching. - No, we're not!
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about outdoor bathing
jokes.punchline: Flanders boys' immediate denial reveals they were watching
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with the kids' immediate guilty response

jokes.text: Benjamin Franklin once said that house guests are like fish at- at- Huh? They're gone! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer preparing to deliver wisdom about overstaying guests
jokes.punchline: Realizes the problem has solved itself mid-quote
jokes.explanation: Good interruption comedy with Homer's relief cutting off his attempted wisdom

jokes.text: Thanks for letting us crash in your pad. We had a very nice time. P.S. We've taken your old lady. - D'oh!
jokes.setup: Polite thank you note from the gang
jokes.punchline: Casual kidnapping announcement
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of polite manners with criminal behavior

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry. You're completely safe. None of us find you sexually attractive. - None of you? Really?
jokes.setup: Gang leader reassuring Marge about her safety
jokes.punchline: Marge's wounded vanity about not being attractive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Marge's vanity overrides safety concerns

jokes.text: 'Korean Love Brides'? - I just don't want to be alone!
jokes.setup: Homer giving Lisa a backup plan for replacing Marge
jokes.punchline: His desperate admission of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's pathetic dependency

jokes.text: To the Bikers' Jamboree in South Dakota. You'll love it. Mickey Rourke's coming, and we're gonna jump him.
jokes.setup: Gang leader describing their destination
jokes.punchline: Casual plan to assault a celebrity
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor with dated celebrity reference

jokes.text: Oh, man. To get all that, you'd have to kill, like, 50 people. - No. You don't have to kill anyone, not if you have jobs.
jokes.setup: Marge describing the benefits of normal life
jokes.punchline: Gang member's assumption that murder is required for success
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing the gang's skewed worldview

jokes.text: No, I believe it's pronounced 'resume.' - Actually, both are acceptable.
jokes.setup: Discussion about resume pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction during life skills lesson
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about pronunciation debates

jokes.text: Miss Simpson, I killed my pencil. - 'Broke.' You broke your pencil. - I broke him.
jokes.setup: Biker struggling with non-violent language
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing the pencil while learning
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing gradual language change

jokes.text: No! My wife is not a doobie to be passed around! I took a sacred vow on my wedding day to bogart her forever!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his marriage
jokes.punchline: Using drug terminology to describe marital commitment
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining romantic devotion with drug slang - highly quotable

jokes.text: Oh! I just swept the circle of death.
jokes.setup: Dramatic fight venue being prepared
jokes.punchline: Marge's domestic concern about cleanliness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's housekeeping instincts override context

jokes.text: You know what I'm gonna do after I kill ya? Take your wallet. - Never! It was a gift from Newsweek!
jokes.setup: Threat of robbery after murder
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre source of wallet pride
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist detail - Newsweek wallet gift is wonderfully specific

jokes.text: A woman like that only comes along every couple of miles.
jokes.setup: Gang leader complimenting Marge
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally suggesting she's not that special
jokes.explanation: Decent backhanded compliment humor - appears romantic but isn't

jokes.text: Hey, can we at least keep her till the orgy in San Berdoo? - What do you say, honey? - No! - No dice!
jokes.setup: Gang member's inappropriate request
jokes.punchline: Homer actually considering it and asking Marge's opinion
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from Homer's clueless consideration of the request

jokes.text: We'll stop at the nearest town and mail our 'resumes.' - 'Resumes.' It still sounds weird to me.
jokes.setup: Gang leaving with their new life skills
jokes.punchline: Still struggling with pronunciation after the lesson
jokes.explanation: Mild callback showing incomplete character development
